change_summary: EXP-433 P1+P2 verification: decider commit 306e025 applied two TLDR-surface patches to close the c6 parallel-surface gaps. P1 (tldr_verdict) replaced "The asymmetry ratio comes from core fluid dynamics, modeled from first principles" with "is reproduced by core-fluid-dynamics simulations from independently measured seismic-tomography inputs (Aubert et al. 2013) — the qualitative asymmetry emerges from independent input data, but the specific 3:1 is not a first-principles prediction in either model." P2 (tldr_evidence) replaced "adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry" with "provides no published source for that specific value and no derivation from dome geometry — either adopted post-hoc from observation or asserted by fiat." Both patches landed in data/wins.json; docs/index.html rebuilt; tests 3692/3692 pass.
our_argument_summary: The dome's 3:1 magnetic decay asymmetry ratio is explained by standard physics (reversed-flux patches in the outer core, reproduced qualitatively by MHD geodynamo simulations from independent seismic-tomography boundary conditions; Aubert et al. 2013). The specific 3:1 ratio is not a first-principles prediction in either standard physics or the dome — but the dome's problem is the absence of any derivation path from its four canonical parameters to that value (either adopted post-hoc from observation or asserted by fiat). Verdict: Std Model Explains. Failure under post-hoc test: indexed public datasets (CHAOS-7, IGRF-14) predate dome adoption; no timestamped pre-registration of the 3:1 figure exists.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py snapshot in raw-text/ corpus contains an asymmetry-ratio domain or fetch for this WIN; the dome paragraph specifies 3:1 vs globe ~1.0x but provides no formula relating any canonical dome parameter to 3:1.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No automation surface — the WIN exists as a written claim only.
data_sources_used: implicit reliance on CHAOS-7 / IGRF for the 3:1 figure though dome paragraph does not cite either by name
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome paragraph never specifies (a) source for 3:1, (b) averaging window, (c) flat-disc-vs-globe hemispheric definition, (d) mechanism. Each unspecified parameter is an implicit escape clause: any observed ratio can be made to fit by selecting a window/definition retroactively.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — our code_analysis_tags accurately reflect the absence of any monitor surface for this WIN and the absence of any derivation path from dome geometry to 3:1. No change since c6.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py snapshot in raw-text/ corpus contains an asymmetry-ratio domain or fetch for this WIN. Verdict text appropriately hedged: "no asymmetry-ratio domain appears in any monitor.py snapshot in our raw-text/ corpus." Page-hash byte-identical for 34+ consecutive polls (raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt header) corroborates no recent monitor-surface change. No change since c5/c6.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Hemispheric/SAA decay asymmetry is described and explained in peer-reviewed literature: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020, doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9); Olson & Deguen 2012 (doi:10.1038/ngeo1506); Aubert et al. 2013 (doi:10.1038/nature12574). The dome renames the cause (toroidal aetheric return-path through SAA shoulder) without quantitative derivation. No change since c5/c6.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 published 2020; IGRF-14 cycle published 2024. Dome WIN-063 dates from V51-era (2026-04 cluster). Indexed public datasets predate dome adoption — fails Part 3.1b legitimate-novelty carve-out. No timestamped pre-registration found. No change since c5/c6.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site maps any of {disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, sun_altitude=5,733 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km} to specifically 3:1 (rather than 2:1 or 5:1). The dome stated mechanism (sub-terrestrial return path through SAA shoulder) is qualitative; H(r) is axisymmetric in longitude; transient sun/moon/aether positions average to azimuthal symmetry over secular timescales. No change since c5/c6.

holes_found.description: tldr_verdict opening compression tension. New opening reads "The asymmetry ratio is reproduced by core-fluid-dynamics simulations from independently measured seismic-tomography inputs (Aubert et al. 2013) — the qualitative asymmetry emerges from independent input data, but the specific 3:1 is not a first-principles prediction in either model." The first clause says "the asymmetry ratio is reproduced" then the second clause clarifies "the qualitative asymmetry emerges... but the specific 3:1 is not a first-principles prediction." A pedantic dome defender could quote the first clause in isolation and claim self-contradiction with the second. The detail_verdict_text wording is cleaner: "The asymmetry phenomenon is a consequence of reversed-flux patches in the outer core, reproduced by MHD geodynamo simulations from independently measured CMB boundary conditions..." — using "phenomenon" rather than "ratio" as the subject of "is reproduced." Severity is minor because: (a) the qualifier rebuts the over-strict reading within the same sentence, (b) the dilemma framing in the subsequent sentence carries the substantive argument, and (c) the c6 holes (the actual high-visibility risks) are closed. This is a polish-only finding, not a defender-exploitable hole on the level of the c5/c6 strawman/overclaim risks.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional next-cycle polish: replace tldr_verdict opening "The asymmetry ratio is reproduced by core-fluid-dynamics simulations" with "The asymmetric decay is reproduced by core-fluid-dynamics simulations" — mirrors detail_verdict_text's "phenomenon"/"qualitative asymmetric decay" framing. NOT BLOCKING. Decider can defer until coupled with a future polish patch; standalone patching this is marginal value-add.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's EXP-433-P1 / EXP-433-P2 patches closed both c6 parallel-surface gaps (data/wins.json:WIN-063.tldr_evidence and data/wins.json:WIN-063.tldr_verdict, verified verbatim 2026-05-26). The two-patch approach is correct: P1 ports the dilemma framing into the high-visibility verdict TLDR, eliminating the self-contradiction with detail_verdict_text's post-940be96 wording. P2 sweeps the parallel CHAOS-7 strawman from the evidence TLDR using the matching dilemma framing already in detail_evidence (post-940be96). The decider correctly recognized the parallel-surface pattern (detail_evidence:c4 → detail_verdict_text+tldr_verdict:c5 → tldr_evidence:c6) and closed the last gap. The EXP itself (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-433-WIN-063-defense.json) is well-documented with cross_page_consistency_after_patches enumerated across 8 surfaces — surfaces 5/6/7/8 (other WINs, sections.json, docs/index.html, PDF) were correctly identified as no-edit-required or rebuild-only.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Closing the parallel-surface gaps does not affect WIN-063's verdict. Verdict remains Std Model Explains (data/wins.json:WIN-063.verdict, unchanged across c5/c6/c7). The patches are defensive — they harden the existing argument against the c5 advocate_mode defense_survives=3 attack surface — but they don't add new substantive critique. The underlying scientific position (no derivation path from dome geometry to 3:1; standard physics qualitatively reproduces the asymmetry from independent seismic inputs; the 3:1 specifically is not a first-principles prediction in either model) is unchanged. Patches improved TLDR accuracy and removed defender-exploitable wording; they did not change what we say.

deeper_analysis:
VERIFICATION OF c6 PATCHES — DETAILED FINDINGS:

(1) c6 Hole 0 (tldr_evidence CHAOS-7 strawman): CLOSED. Verbatim search of data/wins.json:WIN-063.tldr_evidence returns zero hits for "adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation" (the c6-flagged strawman wording). Current text uses dilemma framing "provides no published source for that specific value and no derivation from dome geometry — either adopted post-hoc from observation or asserted by fiat" — matches detail_verdict_text post-940be96 framing. EXP-433-P2 applied as documented.

(2) c6 Hole 1 (tldr_verdict first-principles overclaim): CLOSED. Verbatim search of data/wins.json:WIN-063.tldr_verdict returns zero hits for "modeled from first principles" (the c6-flagged overclaim wording). Full-corpus scan across {claim, finding, tldr_*, detail_*, detail_extra}: zero hits for the overclaim pattern. The phrase "first-principles" (hyphenated) survives in tldr_verdict and detail_verdict_text — but only in the negation "is not a first-principles prediction in either model." The negation is the correct framing. EXP-433-P1 applied as documented; tldr_verdict opening now mirrors detail_verdict_text. Self-contradiction between TLDR and detail (c6 H1's second attack vector) is resolved.

(3) c6 Hole 2 (Olson & Deguen 2012 citation in detail_evidence): UNCHANGED (deferred). Detail_evidence still jointly attributes Olson & Deguen 2012 + Aubert et al. 2013 for "PREM/AK135 reference models, mantle-tomography heat-flux maps." EXP-433-P1 partially addressed H2 in tldr_verdict by citing only Aubert et al. 2013 (which c6 H2 recommended). detail_evidence still carries the joint attribution; this remains the H2 risk surface and remains deferred per c5/c6 (low priority, network access for DOI verification still required).

(4) DOME RAW-TEXT VERIFICATION: raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt (page-hash byte-identical for 34+ consecutive polls per the header) contains the dome's WIN-063 trailing paragraph: "Dome vs Globe: A symmetric globe dipole requires approximately equal decay rates at conjugate latitudes (~1.0x ratio). A 3:1 asymmetry is structurally incompatible with symmetric core flow. The Toroidal Ovoid predicts amplified southern decay because the sub-terrestrial return path exits preferentially through the SAA shoulder." Verbatim grep confirms zero CHAOS-7 mentions in this paragraph. The strawman fix is empirically grounded: the dome does not attribute 3:1 to CHAOS-7, so our prior wording "adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation" was indeed a defender-exploitable misattribution.

(5) CROSS-SURFACE CHAOS-7 CONSISTENCY: All remaining CHAOS-7 mentions in WIN-063 surfaces describe CHAOS-7 accurately as a geomagnetic field model (Finlay et al. 2020). No surface attributes the dome's 3:1 value to CHAOS-7. detail_evidence:"CHAOS-7 as the source for 3:1 — whether the 3:1 was adopted post-hoc from published observation or asserted by fiat (no derivation path given), neither constitutes an independent prediction from dome geometry; the dilemma forecloses both escape routes" — this is the canonical dilemma framing and is well-anchored. detail_verdict_text:"CHAOS-7 describes the field; geodynamo theory explains it; the dome merely relabels it." — accurate three-step framing.

(6) BUILD + TEST: tests/run.sh equivalent (node test.js) reports 3692/3692 passing post-patch. docs/index.html rebuilt — verbatim grep confirms new wording present, old wording absent. PDF regeneration handled by standard build flow.

(7) OTHER-SIDE CHECK (per user preference): Could a dome defender legitimately attack the patched TLDRs? (a) "You say ratio is reproduced then admit 3:1 isn't first-principles" — minor wording tension; qualifier in same sentence rebuts; flagged as Hole 0 (minor). (b) "Your dilemma ignores our qualitative mechanism" — TLDR explicitly acknowledges "qualitative mechanism (toroidal return-path through the SAA shoulder) with no quantitative derivation" in the following sentence; rebut handled. (c) "You don't credit our toroidal model's qualitative claim" — verdict is about quantitative derivation, not qualitative mechanism; framing is fair. Patches are defensible; no new defender-exploitable surface introduced.

(8) EXP-433 SCOPE-NOTE ARTIFACT: The expansion file flags an issue_ids artifact — "ISS-2201-tracker-ref" is a placeholder because ISS-2201 was actually closed and is about WIN-020 c6 (Saros/nodal conflation), not WIN-063. The substantive work was unambiguous from target field, curmudgeon_review reference, and category=defense_neutralization. Decider should clean up the issue_ids field at next integration touchpoint — likely a successor ISS or re-opened ISS-2120 tracking the parallel-surface sweep. Not blocking.

(9) SUMMARY: Both c6 moderate holes CLOSED, c6 minor hole (H2 Olson & Deguen) DEFERRED as before, one new minor wording-polish opportunity flagged (Hole 0 in c7). Verdict holds. Patches are defensible. Verification complete.

citations_verified: Aubert et al. 2013 — cited in tldr_verdict and detail_verdict_text and detail_evidence; doi:10.1038/nature12574 (carried forward from prior cycles, not re-verified this run due to network scope).
CHAOS-7 / Finlay et al. 2020 — cited in detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text; doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 (carried forward).
raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt — page-hash byte-identical for 34+ consecutive polls; dome paragraph contains zero CHAOS-7 mentions (verified verbatim 2026-05-26).
citations_unchecked: Olson & Deguen 2012 (doi:10.1038/ngeo1506) — H2 deferred per c5/c6 recommendations; network DOI resolution required for methodology verification (PREM/AK135 + mantle-tomography input claim). Low priority; Aubert et al. carries the seismic-inputs argument on its own.

summary_for_decider: WIN-063 c7 verification of EXP-433 P1+P2: BOTH c6 MODERATE HOLES CLOSED. (1) tldr_evidence CHAOS-7 strawman → closed by P2. (2) tldr_verdict first-principles overclaim → closed by P1. Self-contradiction between TLDR and detail_verdict_text resolved. Verdict Std Model Explains holds. recommended_action=no_change. One MINOR new finding (Hole 0): tldr_verdict opening "The asymmetry ratio is reproduced" is slightly looser than detail_verdict_text's "phenomenon"/"qualitative asymmetric decay" framing — defender-exploitable only in pedantic isolation; qualifier in same sentence rebuts. Recommend deferring as optional polish; standalone patch is marginal. H2 (Olson & Deguen joint attribution in detail_evidence) UNCHANGED, deferred as before pending network DOI verification. EXP-433 scope_note artifact (issue_ids placeholder "ISS-2201-tracker-ref" misnamed — ISS-2201 actually closed/unrelated) — decider should clean up issue_ids field at next integration touchpoint. Tests 3692/3692; docs/index.html rebuilt verbatim-correct. No carry-forward unresolved_prior_cycle entries (c6 H0/H1 both closed; c6 H2 deferred without ISS, not re-flagged as carry-forward). Singleton review, not batched.
